title: The Little Bear Who Listened with His Heart
moral: True friends don't just hear words — they listen with their hearts and try to understand how others feel.
moralZh: 真正的朋友不只是听见话语，而是用心倾听，尝试理解对方的感受。

vocabulary.meaning: 感到宽慰的，松了口气的

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: In a cozy forest village, there lived a little bear named Bruno. Bruno was big and cheerful, and he loved to play with his best friend — a small rabbit named Rosie.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: One sunny morning, Bruno ran to Rosie's house with a big smile on his face.

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Rosie! Rosie! Let's go to the pond today! I learned how to skip stones! It's SO much fun!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Rosie sat quietly on her porch, hugging her little blanket. She didn't look up.

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "I don't want to go today, Bruno."

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Aww, come on! The water is so sparkly! You'll love it!"

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "I said I don't want to go."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Bruno felt a little confused. He had never seen Rosie like this before. He was about to say "Fine, I'll go alone!" — but then he stopped. He looked at Rosie again. Her eyes were red, and her ears were drooping low.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Something inside Bruno whispered: 'Wait. She looks really upset.'

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Rosie... are you okay?"

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "No... not really."

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "What happened? Can you tell me?"

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "Yesterday, everyone played a game at school. But no one asked me to join. I felt... really lonely."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Bruno was quiet for a moment. He tried to imagine how that must have felt — sitting alone while everyone laughed and played without him. It felt like a cold wind blowing through his heart.

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Oh, Rosie. That sounds really hard. I would feel sad too, if that happened to me."

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "Really? You understand?"

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Yes. Being left out hurts. I'm sorry that happened to you, Rosie."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Rosie's eyes grew wide. She had expected Bruno to say "Don't worry, it's fine!" or "Let's just go play!" But instead, he just... understood. Suddenly, she felt a little lighter.

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "Thank you, Bruno. That makes me feel better."

paragraphs.speaker: Bruno
paragraphs.text: "Do you want to sit together for a while? We don't have to go anywhere."

paragraphs.speaker: Rosie
paragraphs.text: "Actually... maybe a walk would be nice now. Thank you for listening, Bruno."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: As they walked through the sunny forest together, Bruno realized something important: sometimes, the best thing you can do for a friend isn't to fix their problem — it's to truly listen and let them know you understand.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And that is called empathy — listening not just with your ears, but with your whole heart.
